Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 68 actions; a strong score needs about 16 or fewer. You are 4.2x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 10 actions returned the board to a state already seen 10 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 10 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
REVISIT WASTE: this board state has recurred 12 times recently — you are cycling back through states you have already visited. Break the loop and commit to a new line of play.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current step is 93, red at (28, 37)
# Red block pattern: every 5 steps at (28, 31): 86, 91, 96, ...
# UP, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT reaches (28, 31) at step 97
# At step 97, red should be at (28, 37) (97 != 96)

result = action(['UP', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
print("Result:", result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

for node in nodes:
    boundary = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
    cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
    center_row = (min(rows) + max(rows)) // 2
    center_col = (min(cols) + max(cols)) // 2
    
    if node['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"Player (b): center ({center_row}, {center_col})")
    elif node['color'] == 'R':
        print(f"Red (R): center ({center_row}, {center_col})")
    elif node['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green (N): center ({center_row}, {center_col})")

print("Game over:", result.get('game_over', 'N/A'))
print("Executed count:", result.get('executed_count', 'N/A'))
print("Step:", current_frame.step)
print("Level completed:", result.get('level_completed', 'N/A'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
